raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Evoking a moral-philosophical insight that frames suffering as an inner, correctable misalignment rather than an external given, inviting reflective self-examination.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "All suffering possesses a single originating mechanism (thought).",
    "Thought can be categorically 'wrong' or 'right'.",
    "Correction of thought is always available and sufficient to alter suffering.",
    "No suffering arises independently of mental direction."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent as an absolute causal monism; coherence weakens only if external referents (bodily injury, social violence) are imported.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The audience treats the claim as a prompt for examining attitudes and narratives rather than as an empirical diagnosis, and applies it in contexts where mindset modification yields measurable relief.",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statement is read as a literal causal law, leading individuals to attribute all pain (including structural or physiological) to personal cognitive failure and to suppress acknowledgment of contingent external factors."
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: Evoking a moral-philosophical insight that frames suffering as an inner, correctable misalignment rather than an external given, inviting reflective self-examination.
implicit_claims: All suffering possesses a single originating mechanism (thought).
Correction of thought is always available and sufficient to alter suffering.
No suffering arises independently of mental direction.
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: Internally consistent as an absolute causal monism; coherence weakens only if external referents (bodily injury, social violence) are imported.
parsed_json.conditions_for_usefulness: The audience treats the claim as a prompt for examining attitudes and narratives rather than as an empirical diagnosis, and applies it in contexts where mindset modification yields measurable relief.
parsed_json.conditions_for_misleading: The statement is read as a literal causal law, leading individuals to attribute all pain (including structural or physiological) to personal cognitive failure and to suppress acknowledgment of contingent external factors.
